body_txt: When the audience for a broadcast or performance are invited to participate in some way. This can be simple, like a character in a stage show directly asking for the audience's help, or more complex, such as: This trope is a regular feature of Pantomime and usually involves Breaking the Fourth Wall , but if it is done very often that you think the work relies on Audience Participation, then there is No Fourth Wall to begin with. A Super-Trope to Official Fan-Submitted Content , which is when Audience Participation is used to turn fanon into canon; Audience Participation Song , which is when this trope is used in a song; Fake Interactivity , which is when this will have no effect whether the audience chooses to participate or not; and Interactive Comic , which is when a comic is reliant on this trope. Depending on the audience, the Subversion , Audience Participation Failure , can happen. Also contrast The Heckler , someone from the audience who rudely interrupts. Compare NPC Battle Audience , for when an in-universe audience reacts to and interacts with an in-universe bout.
